name: Kentucky Derby
description: The Run for the Roses — America's most famous horse race, run since 1875.

questions.q: Where is the Kentucky Derby held each year?

source.original_a: Churchill Downs in Louisville, Kentucky, US

questions.q: On what day is the Kentucky Derby traditionally held?
What is the traditional distance of the Kentucky Derby?

source.notes: Also called the classic 'mile and a quarter' distance.

questions.q: In what year was the first Kentucky Derby run?
What is the popular nickname for the Kentucky Derby?

source.notes: Refers to the blanket of roses draped on the winner; also called 'The Most Exciting Two Minutes in Sports'.

questions.q: Which Triple Crown leg is the Kentucky Derby?
choices: It is not part of the Triple Crown

source.notes: Followed by the Preakness Stakes (2 weeks later) and the Belmont Stakes (3 weeks later).

questions.q: Which horse won the inaugural Kentucky Derby in 1875?
What is the official traditional cocktail of the Kentucky Derby?

source.notes: Roughly 120,000 are sold over Derby weekend.

questions.q: What song is traditionally played as the horses parade onto the track for the Derby?
Which horse holds the all-time fastest Kentucky Derby time, set in 1973?

source.notes: Time: 1:59 2/5. Secretariat went on to win the Triple Crown that year.

questions.q: Who was the first U.S. Triple Crown winner, in 1919?
What is the Kentucky Oaks?
choices: The race held the day before the Derby for fillies
The bourbon brand sponsoring the Derby
A trophy awarded to the winning trainer

source.notes: Run on the Friday before Derby Saturday for three-year-old fillies.

questions.q: Who was the first female jockey to ride in the Kentucky Derby (in 1970)?
Which jockey, the only rider to win the Triple Crown twice, is tied with Bill Hartack for most Kentucky Derby wins (5)?

source.notes: Tied with Bill Hartack at 5. Arcaro is the only jockey to win the Triple Crown twice (Whirlaway 1941, Citation 1948). audit-corrected 2026-05-09: removed Bill Hartack distractor (also 5 wins) and tightened stem to make Arcaro uniquely correct.

questions.q: Which horse won the 2018 Triple Crown after taking the Derby?

source.notes: Trained by Bob Baffert; ridden by Mike Smith.

questions.q: Which 80-1 long-shot pulled off one of the biggest upsets in Derby history in 2022?

source.notes: Second-highest odds of any Derby winner ever (Donerail won at 91-1 in 1913).

questions.q: How many horses typically start the Kentucky Derby?

source.notes: 20 is the maximum field size; rare years have fewer.

questions.q: What was the longest-odds Derby winner in history (91-to-1, 1913)?
Which Kentucky Derby was famously postponed because of World War II?

source.notes: Run on June 9, 1945 instead of the first Saturday in May.

questions.q: What does the winning horse traditionally have draped over its shoulders in the Winner's Circle?

source.notes: About 400 long-stem red roses are sewn into the blanket each year.
